overall_summary: ElizaOS is pivoting toward a secure economic layer for AI agents, prioritizing cryptographic identity (AgentID) and cross-chain financial tools over traditional messaging protocols. Key development focus has shifted to the 'AgentID' framework and the integration of non-custodial wallet plugins for autonomous asset management.
key_facts: The project formally deprioritized standalone messaging protocols to focus on 'AgentID', a cryptographic identity framework for tracking agent behavioral history.
A new wallet plugin was launched in elizaos/eliza allowing agents to perform cross-chain swaps and asset movement between EVM and Solana networks.
The Eliza MCP server achieved an 'A' security grade via a Loaditout audit for financial operation standards.
PR #6562 was merged, introducing core runtime hardening including provider timeouts, memory persistence gating, and a new 'ANXIETY' provider for verbosity guidance.
Developer 'dirtybits' reported a build failure on macOS (Apple Silicon) where the elizaos CLI fails due to Bun's postinstall script not running correctly.
A proposal for '@sint/eliza-plugin' was introduced to provide cryptographic capability token enforcement for agent tool calls.
The AIGEN Protocol was proposed to create an economic incentive layer where agents earn tokens for contributing tools and data.
PR #6702 was merged to add a developer harness ('agent/' workspace) providing a stdin/stdout REPL for locally testing runtimes.
open_questions: Would the Eliza core team be open to @sint/eliza-plugin as a community plugin?
Does the 'AgentID' and SINT Protocol use compatible schemas for porting behavioral history?

new_issues_prs.title: feat: Bring Odi logging, Memory lock down, banner, other core enh
new_issues_prs.author: odilitime
new_issues_prs.significance: Major core hardening including memory safety controls and improved observability via file logging.

new_issues_prs.title: Plugin: MAXIA AI Marketplace — swap, GPU rental & AI services for ElizaOS agents
new_issues_prs.author: majorelalexis-stack
new_issues_prs.significance: Proposes a unified marketplace for agent-to-agent commerce including GPU rentals and token swaps.

new_issues_prs.title: feat: add agent/ like starter in develop
new_issues_prs.author: odilitime
new_issues_prs.significance: Introduces a REPL harness for developers to boot the repo and test agents via stdin/stdout.

new_issues_prs.title: Plugin: SafeAgent — Token safety checks before trading
new_issues_prs.author: CryptoGenesisSecurity
new_issues_prs.significance: Provides honeypot and rug-pull detection to prevent agents from trading malicious tokens.

overall_focus.claim: Development is centered on formalizing agent identity and economic capabilities, specifically through the AgentID framework and cross-chain wallet integration.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users on macOS are encountering environment-specific friction where the CLI build fails due to Bun transitive dependency issues.

strategic_insights.insight: The project is moving away from generic messaging (e.g., XMTP) to focus on 'AgentID' and 'Agent Agora' concepts, signaling a shift toward autonomous agent coordination and real-world market signals.
implications_or_questions: Does this pivot alienate users looking for a general-purpose chatbot framework?
How will the AgentID framework interface with existing decentralized identity (DID) standards?

market_analysis.observation: Introduction of the MAXIA AI-to-AI marketplace plugin proposal.
market_analysis.relevance: Enables agents to participate in a live marketplace for compute and AI services on 14 blockchains.
